per_turn_leaks.patient_response: It happens pretty much every time I go to the bathroom now, which is 8-10 times a day. Sometimes it's worse than others - there have been days where I've had to change my clothes multiple times because of accidents.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
To be honest, my diet has been pretty irregular lately due to long work hours and stress. I usually end up eating takeout or grabbing something quick on the go, which isn't great for me. As for exercise, I used to try to stay active by going for runs or lifting weights, but with these symptoms, it's been tough to keep that up. I've been trying to get in some yoga or meditation to help manage my stress and alleviate some of the joint pain, but it's not as consistent as I'd like it to be.

I've also noticed that when I do eat, it tends to trigger more diarrhea episodes, especially if I consume anything spicy or fatty. It's like my stomach is super sensitive right now, and even normal foods can set me off.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, so there is a family history of inflammatory bowel disease on my mom's side.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Do you have any allergies to medications?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, I don't have any known drug allergies. I'm actually concerned about potential interactions between my stress medication and the ibuprofen I've been taking for joint pain, though - it seems like the ibuprofen makes my abdominal symptoms worse.
